publications.ref: Peng H, Xu Q, Zhang T, Zhu J, Pan J, Guan X, Feng S, Wu J, Hu Q. Molecular determinants for the association of human hormone-sensitive lipase with lipid droplets.  Nat Commun. 2025 16(1):3497

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No labeling methods mentioned in abstract, consistent with MSE acquisition

Characteristics[CellType].reasoning: Cellular assays mentioned but specific cell type not identified
Characteristics[CellType].needs: Materials and methods section to identify specific cell types used

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a structural biology study of human hormone-sensitive lipase using cryo-EM and mass spectrometry. The organism is confirmed as human, with cellular assays performed but specific cell lines not identified in the abstract. The study appears to use label-free proteomics with MSE acquisition method to analyze lipid droplet binding mechanisms.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: HDX-MS experiment uses pepsin digestion: 'run over an Enzymate BEH Pepsin column', but this is for HDX-MS analysis not sample preparation for proteomics

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 150 mM NaCl, 5% Glycerol, 1 mM DTT... 10 mM DTT, 50 mM MgCl2

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No chemical labeling reagents mentioned in sample preparation. HDX uses deuterium but this is for HDX-MS analysis, not proteomics labeling

Comment[EnrichmentMethod].evidence: No specific enrichment methods described for proteomics sample preparation

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: No fractionation methods described for proteomics sample preparation

Comment[Separation].evidence: separated by an in-line Acquity BEH C18 UPLC column (Waters, 1.0 × 100 mm, 1.7 μm). The mobile phases were 0.1% formic acid in water (A) and 0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile (B)

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: This HDX-MS study uses DTT as reduction reagent and label-free approach. Pepsin digestion is used for HDX-MS analysis (not proteomics sample prep), with reversed-phase chromatography separation. No alkylation reagents, chemical modifications, enrichment or fractionation methods are described for proteomics sample preparation.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: Mass spectra were acquired on a Synapt XS mass spectrometer (Waters)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: The MSE method was used for peptide fragmentation and mapping (Low trap collision energy of 4 V and High ramp trap collision energy of 20–45 V)

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: The MSE method was used for peptide fragmentation and mapping

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: Mass spectra were acquired on a Synapt XS mass spectrometer (Waters) using an electrospray ionization source

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: The gradient of mobile phase B was increased from 2% to 35% in 6 min

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: with a flow rate of 50 μL/min

Comment[CollisionEnergy].reasoning: HDX-MS uses trap collision energy of 20-45V, but specific NCE values for proteomics fragmentation not clearly stated
Comment[CollisionEnergy].needs: Explicit collision energy values for MS2 fragmentation in proteomics analysis

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry analysis was performed using Synapt XS (Waters) with MSE acquisition method and CID fragmentation. Ion trap MS2 analyzer was used with 10 ppm precursor mass tolerance. LC gradient was 6 minutes at 50 μL/min flow rate. Fragment mass tolerance and missed cleavages parameters were not specified in the HDX-MS focused methodology.

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: HDX-MS analysis shows no fractionation described - 'digested peptic peptides were trapped into a VanGuard precolumn and subsequently separated by an in-line Acquity BEH C18 UPLC column'

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].reasoning: Raw file names contain technical replicates (_01, _02, _03) but no clear biological replicate indicators. HDX-MS typically uses technical replicates of the same biological sample
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].needs: Confirmation whether multiple biological samples were used or only technical replicates

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Raw file analysis reveals this is HDX-MS experiment comparing HSL protein with and without ALDs (artificial lipid droplets) at different time points (0, 5, 30, 600 seconds). Files show technical replicates (_01, _02, _03) and two conditions (HSL alone vs HSL_ALDS), but no fractionation, biological replicates, or factor values are evident from the methodology or filenames.
